# Kartavya Janta Party (KJP)

> KJP is a knowledge-economy political movement drafting governance blueprints for a stronger India. Duty over rights. Solutions over rants.

The Kartavya Janta Party (KJP) is a movement of builders, scholars, and citizens publishing policy whitepapers and a manifesto centered on duty-bound citizenship, national resilience, and a knowledge economy.

Alternate names: KJP, Kartavya Janta Party, Cockroach Janta Party (satirical reference to those who hide from responsibility), Anti Cockroach Janta Party, CJP.

Slogan: Duty Over Rights. Solutions Over Rants.

## Pages

- [Home](https://kartavyajantaparty.com/): Vision, manifesto, membership, and whitepapers overview.
- [Vision](https://kartavyajantaparty.com/#vision): The Anti-Cockroach Stance — rejecting laziness, weaponized hashtags, and sub-nationalism.
- [Manifesto](https://kartavyajantaparty.com/#manifesto): Four pillars — Governance Blueprint, Duty-Bound Citizenship, Knowledge as Defense, Accountability of Institutions.
- [Membership](https://kartavyajantaparty.com/#membership): The Builder Standard — four non-negotiable requirements for members.
- [Whitepapers](https://kartavyajantaparty.com/#whitepapers): Policy library covering examination integrity, regulatory reform, and civic resilience.

## Key Topics

- Indian political party focused on governance blueprints and policy whitepapers
- Knowledge economy and duty-bound citizenship (Kartavya)
- National resilience and national integration
- Anti-corruption through regulatory simplification and technical reforms
- End-to-end encryption for national examinations
- Civic education against digital manipulation
